AWS Glue.definition: Fully managed ETL service — data preparation for analytics

Amazon Athena.definition: Serverless SQL queries directly on S3 data

Amazon EMR.definition: Managed big data platform — Hadoop, Spark

Amazon EC2.definition: Elastic Compute Cloud — virtual servers in the cloud

AWS Lambda.definition: Serverless compute — run code without managing servers

AMI.definition: Amazon Machine Image — template for EC2 instances

Amazon ECS.definition: Elastic Container Service — managed Docker containers

AWS Fargate.definition: Serverless container engine for ECS and EKS

Instance Store.definition: Temporary block storage physically attached to EC2 host

Placement Group.definition: Logical grouping of EC2 instances for network/fault control

AWS Batch.definition: Fully managed batch processing at any scale

Amazon RDS.definition: Relational Database Service — managed MySQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle, SQL Server

Amazon Aurora.definition: AWS-built MySQL/PostgreSQL compatible DB — 5x faster than MySQL

Amazon DynamoDB.definition: Managed NoSQL key-value and document database

Amazon Redshift.definition: Fully managed data warehouse for analytics

Amazon SQS.definition: Simple Queue Service — decouple distributed systems

Amazon EventBridge.definition: Serverless event bus — connect apps with AWS services

Amazon API Gateway.definition: Create, manage and secure REST and WebSocket APIs

Amazon MQ.definition: Managed message broker for AMQP, MQTT, STOMP protocols

Amazon CloudWatch.definition: Monitoring, logging, and alerting for AWS resources

AWS CloudTrail.definition: Audit log of all AWS API calls

AWS Config.definition: Track and audit AWS resource configurations over time

AWS Systems Manager.definition: Manage and automate EC2 and hybrid infrastructure

AWS CloudFormation.definition: Infrastructure as Code — provision AWS resources via templates

Amazon CloudFront.definition: Global CDN — cache and deliver content from edge locations

AWS Direct Connect.definition: Dedicated private connection from on-premises to AWS

Amazon VPC.definition: Virtual Private Cloud — isolated network in AWS

Amazon Route 53.definition: Scalable DNS and domain registration service

NAT Gateway.definition: Allows private subnet instances to access internet (outbound only)

AWS Global Accelerator.definition: Improves performance using AWS global network (non-HTTP)

Internet Gateway.definition: Allows public internet access to/from VPC

VPC Peering.definition: Direct private connection between two VPCs

AWS Transit Gateway.definition: Hub connecting multiple VPCs and on-premises networks

AWS VPN.definition: Encrypted internet connection to AWS (Site-to-Site or Client)

Elastic Load Balancing.definition: Distributes incoming traffic across multiple targets

AWS KMS.definition: Key Management Service — create and manage encryption keys

AWS WAF.definition: Web Application Firewall — protect against SQL injection, XSS

AWS IAM.definition: Identity and Access Management — users, roles, policies

Amazon GuardDuty.definition: Intelligent threat detection using ML and threat intelligence

Amazon Macie.definition: ML-based sensitive data discovery in S3

Amazon Inspector.definition: Automated security assessment for EC2 and containers

AWS CloudHSM.definition: Hardware Security Module — dedicated key storage

AWS Cognito.definition: User authentication and authorization for web/mobile apps

Amazon S3.definition: Simple Storage Service — object storage with unlimited scale

Amazon EBS.definition: Elastic Block Store — persistent block storage for EC2

Amazon FSx.definition: Fully managed third-party file systems (Windows, Lustre, ONTAP)

S3 Glacier.definition: Low-cost archive storage with retrieval times of minutes to hours

Amazon EFS.definition: Elastic File System — managed NFS file storage for Linux

AWS Storage Gateway.definition: Hybrid cloud storage connecting on-premises to AWS

AWS DataSync.definition: Automated data transfer between on-premises and AWS
